query: Just FYI... Informs hypothesis is driven through NLI identification... something we've proven thats ineffective.  So any Informs conclusions can only say that this method of identifying this as a graph edge is ineffective/effective... it cannot make that judgement for the concept of Informs as a general rule.  as far as what to check on... Easy enough to start with a fresh snapshot to eliminate the issue altogether.  You need to validate the baseline (nli) to make sure we know what we're comparing against

entries.title: NLI neutral-zone score is the detection signal for cross-category institutional memory edges
ADR-001 col-029: EDGE_SOURCE_NLI Named Constant in unimatrix-store
Graph inference edge stall: supports_edge_threshold too conservative for retrospective-dominated corpus
TypedRelationGraph: cycle detection requires Supersedes-only temp graph; full graph false-positives on CoAccess bidirectional pairs
ADR-001 bugfix-458: GRAPH_EDGES compaction must filter on status != 3 (quarantined), not just entry existence
